refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral, specifying what the system must achieve without prescribing how. Therefore, it is atomic and does not require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to ensure packet loss stays below threshold
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and monitoring feedback loops
Interaction between error correction and retransmission mechanisms
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality control within the protocol design, supporting independence from other system functions. Adaptive error correction and retransmission are closely related but can be modularized to minimize coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and stable enough for adaptive protocols to be effective
Risk that extreme network degradation may exceed protocol capabilities leading to higher packet loss
Assumes that the safety plan and operational policies do not impose constraints that limit protocol adaptability
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time network condition monitoring as a supporting DP or sub-DP to enhance adaptability and robustness of the communication protocol.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network communication stack and protocol implementations responsible for managing data transmission, including adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network layer for sending and receiving packets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides status and performance metrics of the communication protocol to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol logically belongs here; minimal diffusion since protocol logic is centralized.
Low cohesion; communication protocol is not a core vehicle function; would require significant diffusion to other modules.
Some relevance due to remote control, but protocol implementation is more foundational and should be lower-level.
Safety services may use communication but do not implement the protocol; high diffusion and low cohesion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication functions and can encapsulate adaptive error correction and retransmission logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
If protocol requirements change to include higher-level application logic, some diffusion to Teleoperation or Safety Services may occur.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks materialize, consider refining the DP to separate low-level protocol mechanics (in NGHE Solution) from higher-level communication policies (possibly in Teleoperation) to improve modular clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms designed to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limits, fulfilling the teleoperation communication quality requirement.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive with the DP, containing the communication protocol logic centrally with minimal diffusion risk. Other modules show low containment scores and high diffusion, confirming NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface under agreed network conditions to ensure compliance with the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces defined for network communication and telemetry, supporting traceability and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Monitor potential dependencies on lower-level hardware interfaces to ensure full encapsulation within NGHE Solution.

If future protocol requirements expand to include higher-level application logic, consider decomposing the DP to separate low-level protocol mechanics (retained in NGHE Solution) from higher-level communication policies (possibly allocated to Teleoperation) to maintain modular clarity and reduce diffusion risk.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and no significant diffusion risks. The DP fully satisfies the FR with a well-contained IM allocation, and verification is feasible. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable, minimizing complexity and diffusion risk. Recommended revisions are noted for future consideration but do not warrant further decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: Monitor dependencies on lower-level hardware interfaces to ensure full encapsulation within NGHE Solution and consider future decomposition if protocol requirements expand to higher-level application logic.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module fully encapsulates the communication protocol logic.
Packet loss metrics can be reliably monitored via the Telemetry interface under agreed network conditions.
open_risks: Potential dependencies on lower-level hardware interfaces that may affect encapsulation.
Future expansion of protocol requirements that could increase diffusion risk if not managed.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss ≤1% under operational conditions.
Documentation confirming encapsulation of hardware dependencies within NGHE Solution module.
